content.problemStatement:
The king of Berland wants to revise his road system. Again.

This time the kingdom of Berland consists of nnn cities and mmm one-way roads. The cities are numbered from 1 to nnn. Each road allows to travel from some city to some other city. It is not possible to travel the road in the opposite direction.

The king plans to stimulate a migration in his country, so he plans to change the direction of some roads in such a way that there exists a path from city 111 to city nnn. Due to complicated legislation, he is only allowed to perform operations of two types:


Pick some city vvv. Consider all cities uuu such that there exists a road between vvv and uuu (direction doesn't matter) and change its direction to lead from city vvv to city uuu. In other words, all roads incident to city vvv become outgoing.
Pick some city vvv. Consider all cities uuu such that there exists a road between vvv and uuu (direction doesn't matter) and change its direction to lead from city uuu to city vvv. In other words, all roads incident to city vvv become incoming.
The king is a very busy man so he wants to minimize the total number of actions he has to perform, so that at the end, there will be a directed path from 111 to nnn. Help the king by computing this value for him, or determine that there is no solution.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer ttt - the number of test cases in this input. Then follow ttt descriptions of individual test cases.
The first line of each test case description starts with two integers nnn and mmm, denoting the number of cities and the number of roads in the kingdom of Berland.
Then follow mmm lines, the iii-th line containing integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, meaning that there is a road between cities uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​ that is currently directed from uiu_iui​ to viv_ivi​.


Output Format

For each test case, output a single line which should contain a single integer equal to the minimum number of actions the king needs to take in order to create a directed path from city 111 to city nnn.
If there is no solution for some of the test cases, the answer for such test cases should be −1-1−1.
constraints: It is guaranteed that there is no more than one road (whatever direction) between each pair of cities.
It is guaranteed that the total number of cities in all test cases doesn't exceed 500000 and the total number of roads in all test cases doesn't exceed 1000000.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer t - the number of test cases in this input. Then follow t descriptions of individual test cases. The first line of each test case description starts with two integers n and m, denoting the number of cities and the number of roads in the kingdom of Berland. Then follow m lines, the i-th line containing integers u_i and v_i, meaning that there is a road between cities u_i and v_i that is currently directed from u_i to v_i.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line which should contain a single integer equal to the minimum number of actions the king needs to take in order to create a directed path from city 1 to city n. If there is no solution for some of the test cases, the answer for such test cases should be -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The sample input consists of two test cases.
In the first test case, there are no roads at all, so the answer is -1.
In the second test case, the king can pick the 5-th city and change the direction of all roads incident to this city (these are roads 2-5 and 4-5) to point to city 5. Thus, the answer is 1.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a graph and perform a breadth-first search (BFS) to find the shortest path from city 1 to city n. We will also keep track of the minimum number of actions required to create a directed path from city 1 to city n.
solution.timeComplexity: O(t * (n + m)) - where t is the number of test cases, n is the number of cities, and m is the number of roads.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n + m) - where n is the number of cities and m is the number of roads.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
# Uses input() or sys.stdin
# Handles all T test cases
import sys
from collections import deque

# Function to perform BFS
def bfs(adj, start, end):
    n = len(adj)
    dist = [1000000000] * n
    visited = [False] * n
    dist[start] = 0
    visited[start] = True
    q = deque([start])
    while q:
        u = q.popleft()
        for i in range(len(adj[u])):
            if adj[u][i] == 1 and not visited[i]:
                dist[i] = dist[u] + 1
                visited[i] = True
                q.append(i)
    minActions = dist[end]
    return minActions

# Read input
t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n, m = map(int, input().split())
    adj = [[0] * m for _ in range(n)]
    for _ in range(m):
        u, v = map(int, input().split())
        adj[u - 1][v - 1] = 1
    minActions = bfs(adj, 0, n - 1)
    if minActions == 1000000000:
        print(-1)
    else:
        print(minActions)
